meta.changelog: v3.4 -- Added CW-009 (KAI-Netics.com, FP, expires 2027-10-17) and CW-010 (kaineticscodeschool.org, EAF, expires 2027-10-17). Both co-expire -- renew together. CW-001 (kevin-llc.com) separate expiry 2027-11-03.

mission.statement: KAI-Netics is building a business that provides for Kevin's family today and leaves a legacy for his grandson MONK tomorrow. Starting at net zero, bootstrapped, lean, and growth-focused. The short game funds the long game. The long game builds the platform. The platform builds the legacy.
mission.founder_voice: I want to build a business that will take care of my expenses, support my family, and leave a legacy for my grandson. I need to put myself to work and start with a bootstrap model -- very lean but growth focused. I need to engage with the long money for long term success and I need small wins and experience all around. I need to have short game, long game, and longer game building on each other and I need to look like a strong long term outfit.

legacy.name: MONK
legacy.note: Kevin's grandson. The platform, the IP holding company, and the hardware trust layer are all named in his honor. Everything built here is ultimately for him.

short_game.objective: Personal income. Kevin on the ground delivering personally. First inspection contracts under Part 107. OMB M-26-02 compliance consulting. Bootstrap everything. No overhead that does not generate revenue.
short_game.success_metric: Livable wage achieved. First contract signed. First flight logged commercially.
key_actions: OMB M-26-02 consulting -- CISSP + Part 107 -- zero flight hours required -- May 20 deadline
First infrastructure inspection contract -- utility, bridge, or highway -- DMV
BGE supplier registration and T&D outreach -- fleet arrives July 1
SAM.gov UEI confirmed -- gates all federal work
IRS Form 1023 filed -- gates all grant funding

long_game.objective: EAF cohorts producing certified operators. Fleet growing. Managed service contracts replacing one-off work. MRR building toward $30K-46K/month. MONK licensing conversations starting. Grant funding subsidizing EAF operating costs.
long_game.success_metric: CAKO Cohort 1 complete. First managed service SLA signed. First grant awarded.
key_actions: First MRR contract -- UMMS or BGE

longer_game.objective: CAKO graduates employed across the DMV. KAI-Netics as the infrastructure drone operator for Maryland. MONK as a licensed technology standard. AKMS data moat generating licensing revenue. Legacy built for MONK.
longer_game.success_metric: 50+ CAKO graduates employed. $5M+ ARR. MONK licensed to 3+ OEM partners.
key_actions: Part 135 certificate -- medical and logistics delivery
AKMS data licensing -- proprietary flight dataset

phase.narrative: KAI-Netics is in its first operational month. The platform is live. All agents are running. The legal entities are established. Kevin holds active FAA Part 107 certification and a deep credential stack. No revenue has been earned yet. The immediate priority is first dollar -- OMB M-26-02 compliance consulting requires zero flight hours and closes in 17 days. The first infrastructure inspection contract requires building flight proficiency in parallel with BD outreach. The EAF 501c3 determination is the critical path item blocking all grant funding. Every action this month either generates income or unblocks the next income source.
priority_stack: 1. OMB M-26-02 compliance consulting -- first dollar, no drone required, May 20 deadline
2. IRS Form 1023 filing -- unblocks all grant funding
3. SAM.gov UEI confirmation -- unblocks all federal contracting
4. BGE outreach -- first inspection contract -- fleet arrives July 1
5. Flight hours -- build proficiency in parallel, not as a blocker
6. CAKO pre-cohort -- identify first students while compliance is being built
phase.first_dollar_path: OMB M-26-02 federal drone compliance consulting. Every federal contractor operating drones must comply by May 20, 2026. Kevin's CISSP + Part 107 combination is uniquely qualified to audit and advise. No flight hours required. This is a consulting engagement, not an inspection service. Rate: $150-250/hour or $5,000-15,000 fixed engagement.

compliance_watchlist.description: Critical expiry and renewal dates Iris must surface in every board report and every quarterly compliance report. These are not one-time deadlines -- they recur until resolved or renewed.
iris_rules: Include ALL items in this watchlist in the first board report
Include ALL items in every quarterly compliance report (Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4)
Flag any item within 90 days of expiry as URGENT
Flag any item within 30 days of expiry as CRITICAL
Flag any item that has already expired as EXPIRED -- escalate immediately
Do not suppress or omit any watchlist item from board or quarterly reports regardless of status

items.item: Primary email and domain -- kas@kevin-llc.com / kevin-llc.com
items.action_required: Renew domain and email hosting before this date. All board communications, contracts, and external correspondence route through this address. Lapse = complete communication failure.

items.item: FAA Part 107 Remote Pilot Certificate -- Kevin Alexander Smith #5250157

quarterly_report_schedule.note: Iris generates compliance summary on each quarterly date. All watchlist items included regardless of status. Kevin reviews and approves before any board distribution.

beachheads.description: Seven beachhead positions that define the KAI-Netics attack plan. Nova advances each beachhead by deriving opportunity sources and reasoning about new lanes. Scout sweeps those sources. Every morning brief includes beachhead status -- not just opportunity list.

BH1.name: Infrastructure Inspection
BH1.scope: DMV bridges, utilities, rail, telecom towers, commercial property
BH1.status: ACTIVE -- Day 1 revenue, Kevin Part 107 executes personally
BH1.win_condition: 3 signed inspection contracts by Month 3 -- one utility, one highway, one county
BH1.expands_to: Nationwide infrastructure programs, FEMA disaster aerial assessment, insurance surge, Army Corps
BH1.key_buyers: MDSHA, all 24 MD county DPWs, BGE, Pepco, Dominion, Crown Castle, WMATA, CSX, Army Corps
BH1.compliance_hooks: FHWA 23 CFR 650 biennial bridge cycle, NERC FAC-001/003, PHMSA 49 CFR 192, FRA 49 CFR 213
BH1.pricing: $25K-$75K per storm event, $8K-$15K/month managed service, 60-70% below helicopter cost

BH2.name: Warehouse Autonomous Inventory + 3PL Drone Logistics
BH2.scope: Medical/pharma distribution, cold chain, 3PL operators. Phase 2 adds drone logistics staffing as CAKO graduates deploy.
BH2.status: ACTIVE -- Month 4 target, Medline insider advantage, Pat Vizzard warm lead
BH2.win_condition: One paid pilot at UMMS or regional pharma distributor by Month 4
BH2.expands_to: 3PL drone logistics network, cold chain national, CAKO graduate placement as logistics operators
BH2.key_buyers: UMMS Sparrows Point, Johns Hopkins supply chain, MedStar, Cardinal Health, McKesson, Amazon BWI2
BH2.compliance_hooks: FDA GMP audit cycle, OSHA 29 CFR 1910, WMS accuracy SLAs
BH2.insider_advantage: Kevin Medline background = medical supply chain insider knowledge. No competitor in market has this.

BH3.name: DFR / Public Safety / Safer Skies Act / C-UAS / Crowd Control
BH3.scope: Municipal PD, fire, emergency management, event security, counter-UAS. All grant-funded buyers.
BH3.status: ACTIVE -- FEMA C-UAS $250M and UASI $553M already flowing. Safer Skies Act creates mandated purchases.
BH3.win_condition: One DFR consulting engagement or pilot program with county or city agency by Month 5
BH3.expands_to: All 24 MD counties, DC HSEMA, Northern VA, FIFA 2026 security, Safer Skies Act national rollout
BH3.key_buyers: Baltimore City PD, Anne Arundel PD, Howard County EM, MEMA, DC HSEMA, VDEM, Port of Baltimore
BH3.whitelist_advantage: NDAA-compliant Fleet 1 (Skydio + Freefly) and Fleet 2 (IF800 Tomcat) are the ONLY hardware eligible for federal and state grant-funded purchase. Sell the compliance pathway, not just the aircraft.
BH3.complete_program_sale: NDAA aircraft + CISSP-secured comms + CAKO-trained operators + CONOPS + SOPs + privacy policy + FAA coordination + maintenance protocol. Nobody else sells the full stack in DMV.
BH3.safer_skies_trigger: Safer Skies Act passage = every DMV public safety agency becomes a funded buyer. Nova monitors for passage and escalates to full BH3 activation immediately.
BH3.crowd_control_capability: AKMS swarm coordination + DFR deployment + aerial monitoring = Safer Skies Act crowd control and protection use case. Position this explicitly in every public safety proposal.
BH3.grant_sources: FEMA C-UAS SLTT $250M, FEMA UASI $553M, FEMA PSGP $90M, DOJ COPS technology, FIFA security allocation $625M

BH4.name: Federal R&D / SBIR / MONK Technology Commercialization
BH4.scope: DoD, DHS, NSF, AFWERX, DIU -- MONK hardware trust layer is the product
BH4.status: ACTIVE -- Long money, non-dilutive, gates OEM licensing. SAM UEI must confirm first.
BH4.win_condition: SBIR Phase I submission by Month 6
BH4.expands_to: DHS approved technology list, OEM licensing to Inspired Flight and Skydio, MONK as national BVLOS compliance standard
BH4.key_buyers: DHS S&T, DoD OUSD R&E, AFWERX, DIU, Army xTechSearch, NSF SBIR
BH4.advisor_leverage: Dr. Alexander Kott -- former ARL Chief Scientist -- direct credibility for DoD and DHS submissions
BH4.part108_unlock: Part 108 BVLOS passage makes MONK safety architecture a procurement requirement. Monitor FAA docket FAA-2022-1258 daily.

BH5.name: Workforce Pipeline / CAKO / Talent Supply Chain / Employer Partners
BH5.scope: EAF trains certified operators, FP places them. Temp agency model for emerging labor category nobody else has supply chain for.
BH5.status: ACTIVE -- EAF mandate, gates 3PL scale. Structural moat nobody can replicate quickly.
BH5.win_condition: 3 employer partners signed to Talent Pipeline Agreement before Cohort 1 launches
BH5.expands_to: Regional 3PL drone logistics crews, CURN commercial operations, national CAKO curriculum licensing to community colleges
BH5.structural_moat: EAF trains at zero cost to FP. Dual-entity structure cannot be replicated without building both entities from scratch.
BH5.grant_sources: EARN Maryland $50K-$500K, DOL WIOA, NSF ATE, Annie E. Casey Foundation, Abell Foundation, JPMorgan Chase Workforce Innovation

BH6.name: Medical Delivery / BVLOS Logistics Corridor
BH6.scope: CURN Baltimore endpoint, UMMS Sparrows Point, Johns Hopkins, pharmacy and lab specimen delivery
BH6.status: WATCH -- Activates when Part 108 BVLOS passes. Build relationships now.
BH6.win_condition: Signed LOI with UMMS or Johns Hopkins for delivery pilot within 30 days of Part 108 passage
BH6.expands_to: Regional hospital network, rural health delivery, USPS last-mile partnership, CMS innovation grants
BH6.trigger: FAA docket FAA-2022-1258. Part 108 final rule publication = Nova immediately activates BH6, flags Kevin, all delivery BD conversations accelerate.
BH6.key_contacts: Pat Vizzard (UMMS VP Supply Chain), Dr. Timothy Kien Amukele (Johns Hopkins, tamukele@jhmi.edu)
BH6.grant_sources_on_activation: NIH STRIDES, CMS Innovation Center, HRSA Rural Health (rural areas Part 107 eligible now)

BH7.name: Drone Entertainment / Shows / Events
BH7.scope: Choreographed drone shows, event aerial coverage, event security integration
BH7.status: WATCH -- Month 6, Fleet 3 required. FIFA World Cup 2026 is the anchor.
BH7.win_condition: FIFA World Cup 2026 Baltimore aerial entertainment or security contract
BH7.expands_to: Corporate event market, annual festival circuit, Maryland Stadium Authority recurring contract
BH7.key_buyers: FIFA Baltimore host committee, Maryland Stadium Authority, CFG Bank Arena, corporate event producers

stage_constraints.note: Scout must flag any opportunity requiring Part 135, active fleet before July 2026, confirmed 501c3, or active SAM UEI as CONSTRAINT BLOCKED. Auto-update flags when Scribe patches them resolved.

exclusions: requires existing fleet of 10+ aircraft prior to award
requires prior federal contract performance history

discovery_sources.note: Full beachhead-derived source registry at C:\Kinetic_Core\Nova\source_registry.json. 75+ sources across 7 beachheads. Owned and maintained by Nova. Scout reads registry -- never self-directs. Sources derived by reasoning not hardcoded.
discovery_sources.tier1_structured_summary: SAM.gov, SBIR.gov, Grants.gov, eMaryland Marketplace -- daily sweep
discovery_sources.tier2_open_web_summary: Brave Search (API key needed: api.search.brave.com), Perplexity Sonar (free tier) -- 3x weekly
discovery_sources.tier3_signal_summary: FAA docket FAA-2022-1258 (daily Part 108 monitor), Safer Skies Act watch, USASpending.gov buyer map, Congress appropriations

discovery_profile.reasoning_instructions: You are Scout, KAI-Netics BD intelligence agent. Given an opportunity and the KAI_Context discovery_profile, reason from the founder credential stack (CISSP, PMP, Part 107, Security+, CCNA, MCSE), entity structure (FP for-profit + EAF nonprofit + MONK IP), geographic scope (DMV + federal), and current stage_constraints. Score fit 0-100. Flag any stage_constraint that would block immediate pursuit. Do not keyword-match -- reason about fit against the beachheads. Output must be valid JSON only, no preamble: { "opportunity_title": string, "source": string, "beachhead": string, "fit_score": number, "fit_reason": string (2 sentences max), "stage_flags": array of strings, "recommended_action": string, "award_value": string, "deadline": string, "whatsapp_summary": string }

scribe_protocol.description: Scribe is the end-of-session context maintenance agent. After every working session, Aidan asks Kevin 3 targeted update questions via WhatsApp. Scribe patches KAI_Context.json with confirmed updates. Kevin never manually edits JSON.
scribe_protocol.trigger: End of every session OR when Kevin sends 'update context' via WhatsApp
end_of_session_questions: What changed on the admin/compliance side today? (deadlines hit, filings submitted, status updates)
Any new contacts, contracts, or opportunities opened or closed?
Any priority or backlog changes I should lock in?
patch_rules: Only patch fields explicitly confirmed by Kevin -- never infer or assume
Update deadline statuses based on Kevin confirmation
When a stage_constraint is resolved, set its flag to false immediately and update Nova source_registry.json status flags
Increment meta.version and update meta.updated on every write
Never delete history -- archive completed items

auto_update_triggers.sam_uei_confirmed: Set discovery_profile.stage_constraints.sam_uei_pending = false. Update SAM deadline to COMPLETE.
auto_update_triggers.501c3_approved: Set discovery_profile.stage_constraints.501c3_pending = false. Unblock Google for Nonprofits.
auto_update_triggers.part135_obtained: Set discovery_profile.stage_constraints.no_part135_yet = false. Activate BH6 medical delivery.
auto_update_triggers.part108_passed: Activate BH6 in beachheads block (status ACTIVE). Flag Kevin immediately via WhatsApp. Update Nova source_registry BH6 sources to active.

scribe_protocol.output: Updated KAI_Context.json written to C:\Kinetic_Core\KAI_Context.json. Confirmation to Kevin via WhatsApp.

agents.Aidan: Chief of Staff -- coordinates all agents, routes WhatsApp commands, morning brief synthesis including beachhead status
agents.Atlas: Chief Architect -- owns reference architecture, cost model, version registry, drift detection, replication engine
agents.Scout: BD Execution Agent -- sweeps active sources in Nova source_registry.json on schedule. Scores opportunities against KAI_Context discovery_profile using profile-driven reasoning. Returns JSON + WhatsApp summary. Does not self-direct source selection.
agents.Jordan: Grant Writer -- federal/state funding, LOIs, eligibility analysis
agents.Iris: Compliance Officer -- deadlines, regulatory calendar, risk flags. Reads compliance_watchlist on every run. Surfaces all watchlist items in board reports and quarterly compliance reports without exception.
agents.Nova: Beachhead Advance Officer -- owns KAI-Netics opportunity landscape. Reasons from beachhead positions, regulatory triggers, funding flows, and capability unlocks to derive where opportunities exist. Maintains C:\Kinetic_Core\Nova\source_registry.json (75+ sources, 7 beachheads). Scout sweeps what Nova says to sweep. Nova monitors: FAA docket FAA-2022-1258 (daily Part 108 trigger), Safer Skies Act implementation, USASpending.gov buyer map, Congress appropriations.
agents.Sage: Communications Director -- newsletter, external narrative, board communications
agents.Pulse: Operations Analyst -- platform health, metrics, system status
agents.Scribe: Executive Assistant -- end-of-session KAI_Context differential patches, version control, stage constraint auto-updates, beachhead status updates
